steps.title: Identify the central contrast
steps.content: The passage sets up a stark opposition: America is preoccupied with “material interest,” while scholars must represent “the spiritual interest of the world.” The passage calls it a “common calamity” if scholars neglect this duty in such a materialistic country.

steps.title: Apply the contrast to the choices
steps.content: D captures the passage's argument: America's material preoccupation makes it crucial for scholars to foster and preserve intellectual interests. A, B, and C each diverge from this central claim. That matches choice D — D is correct.
